Document:
# Cooking
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Cooking"]
Cooking turns raw wasteland ingredients into meals that are safer, more filling, and usually more valuable than eating ingredients by themselves. Most recipes rely on a heat source plus basic meats, produce, grains, or scavenged pre-war food.
## Cooking Equipment
A cooking stove or kitchen grill is needed for most hot meals.
[bold]Cooking Stove[/bold] — standard heat source for prepared meals
[bold]Kitchen Grill[/bold] — open-flame cooking surface
## Raw Meats
Wasteland creatures provide a wide range of raw meats. [color=#a4885c]Do not eat raw meat[/color] unless you have no other choice.
[bold]Brahmin Meat[/bold]
[bold]Bighorner Meat[/bold]
[bold]Radstag Meat[/bold]
[bold]Radhog Meat[/bold]
[bold]Pigrat Meat[/bold]
[bold]Yaoguai Meat[/bold]
[bold]Radscorpion Meat[/bold]
[bold]Radchicken Meat[/bold]
[bold]Gecko Meat[/bold]
[bold]Fire Gecko Meat[/bold]
[bold]Golden Gecko Meat[/bold]
[bold]Iguana Meat[/bold]
[bold]Mirelurk Claw[/bold]
[bold]Squirrel Meat[/bold]
## Fresh Produce
Plants grown by farmers or gathered from the Wasteland. They can be eaten raw, processed into extracts, or used in more complete meals.
[bold]Carrot[/bold], [bold]Cabbage[/bold], [bold]Corn[/bold], [bold]Onion[/bold], [bold]Potato[/bold], [bold]Tomato[/bold], [bold]Apple[/bold]
## Cooked Meals
Prepared meals made at a cooking station. These are your best everyday food source when a settlement has a working kitchen.
[bold]Ribs[/bold] — hearty grilled meat
[bold]Cuban Carp[/bold] — seasoned carp dish
[bold]Omelette[/bold] — egg-based breakfast
[bold]Fried Egg[/bold] — simple egg dish
[bold]Enchiladas[/bold] — spiced meat wraps
[bold]Nachos[/bold] — tortilla chips with toppings
[bold]Fries[/bold] — deep-fried potato slices
[bold]Queso[/bold] — melted cheese dip
[bold]Sashimi[/bold] — raw fish preparation
[bold]Loaded Potato[/bold] — stuffed baked potato
[bold]Fruit Salad[/bold] — mixed fruit bowl
[bold]Herb Salad[/bold] — fresh herb mix
[bold]Coleslaw[/bold] — shredded cabbage side
## Soups & Bowls
Simple one-pot foods and grain dishes that stretch ingredients further than most plated meals.
[bold]Pea Soup[/bold] — basic nutritious soup
[bold]Boiled Rice[/bold] — plain steamed grain
[bold]Gumbo[/bold] — rice and meat stew
[bold]Rice Pudding[/bold] — sweet grain dessert
[bold]Oatmeal[/bold] — hot grain porridge
## Baked Goods
Require grain or flour, plus a reliable heat source.
[bold]Plain Bread[/bold] — basic wheat loaf
[bold]Corn Bread[/bold] — dense corn flour bread
[bold]Banana Bread[/bold] — sweet fruit bread
[bold]Meat Bread[/bold] — protein-packed savory loaf
[bold]Radroll[/bold] — irradiated bread roll
[bold]Mutfruit Pie[/bold] — classic wasteland pastry
[bold]Blackberry Pie[/bold] — sweet berry pie
[bold]Meat Pie[/bold] — savory meat pastry
## Pre-War Packaged Food
Pre-war food can still be found throughout the Wasteland. It stores well and is useful when you need fast calories without cooking.
[bold]BlamCo Mac & Cheese[/bold] — boxed pasta
[bold]Cram[/bold] — canned mystery meat
[bold]Dandy Boy Apples[/bold] — preserved apple snacks
[bold]Fancy Lads Snack Cakes[/bold] — sugar-packed treats
[bold]InstaMash[/bold] — instant mashed potatoes
[bold]Pork n' Beans[/bold] — canned beans
[bold]Dinki-Di[/bold] — canned dog food
## Cooking Recipes
The in-game crafting menu shows all available food recipes with their ingredients and requirements.
